key_insight: CatRetriever learns a shared latent space for slabs and bulk crystals, enabling accurate retrieval of plausible parent bulk structures from a generated slab query.
engineering_relevance: This bridges generative catalyst design with bulk-level materials screening, helping engineers assess synthesizeability and bulk-dependent properties alongside surface activity.
method: Contrastive representation learning aligns slab and bulk crystal embeddings.
A slab query retrieves top candidate parent bulks from a cataloged bulk space.
An extended pipeline combines retrieval, generative search expansion, and adsorption-energy distribution analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong retrieval performance with R@1 above 91% and R@3 above 98% on both in-distribution and holdout evaluation sets.
watch_item: Retrieval quality depends on the coverage and correctness of the bulk reference space, so novel or poorly represented chemistries may be missed.
paper_title: CatRetriever: Contrastive Representation Learning for Slab-to-Bulk Retrieval in Generative Catalyst Discovery
